_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Conduct survey of computer and digital networks.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-001 (SP, cosine: 0.342), AI-T-003 (AI, cosine: 0.335).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.4/10 (raw 0.542). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [computer, conduct, digital, networks, survey] vs WIDAI-only terms [affects, architecture, balancing, configurations, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.857 (P=0.879, R=0.836). P exceeds R by 0.043 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 31 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Conduct survey of computer and digital networks." and "Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.358 < STS 0.542 (gap -0.185). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [computer, conduct, digital] and [affects, architecture, balancing] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [computer, conduct, digital, networks, survey]. WIDAI terms: [affects, architecture, balancing, configurations, data, design].
